title: On the Security Risks of AutoML
focus: Security implications and vulnerabilities in Neural Architecture Search (NAS)
research_questions: Does NAS introduce new weaknesses, compared with the conventional ML practice?
If so, what are the possible root causes of such vulnerability?
How would ML practitioners mitigate such drawbacks in designing and operating NAS?
key_findings: NAS-generated models tend to suffer greater vulnerability to various malicious manipulations including adversarial evasion, model poisoning, backdoor injection, functionality stealing, and label-only membership inference
NAS tends to prematurely evaluate models before convergence, favoring models that converge fast, resulting in architectural properties that facilitate attacks
Potential remedies include post-NAS mitigation (e.g., adversarial training) and in-NAS strategies (e.g., increasing cell depth)
methodology: Extensive empirical study of 10 representative NAS methods, with both empirical and analytical evidence
